entry.title: SAP UI5 Integration Cards - Beginners Steps ( Understanding of JSON) to transform into Dashboard
entry.content:
Understanding the Manifest.json File: Detailed explanation of the manifest.json file and its key components.

Reference blog : https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-you-can-prepare-your-own-template-using-sap-ui5-integration-cards/ba-p/13881521

Prepare a Sample UI5 Integration Card with lightweight approach referencing your Odata API

index.html

 

 

 

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
	<head>
		<meta http-equiv="X-UA-Compatible" content="IE=edge">
		<meta charset="utf-8">
		<title>Card As Custom Element</title>
		<script src="https://ui5.sap.com/resources/sap-ui-integration.js"
				id="sap-ui-bootstrap"
				data-sap-ui-theme="sap_horizon">
		</script>
		<style>
			.myCard {
				margin: 1rem;
				width: 17rem;
                .sapFGridContainer {
                padding: 12rem;
            }                
			}
			.myTable::after {
				margin: 1rem;
				width: 27rem;
                .sapFGridContainer {
                padding: 12rem;
            }                
			}            
		</style>
	</head>
	<body class="sapUiBody">  
		<ui-integration-card
			badge="New"
			id="cardCustomElement"
			manifest="./manifest.json"
			data-mode="Active"
			base-url="./"
			class="myCard">
		</ui-integration-card>
	</body>
</html>

 

 

 

manifest.json

 

 

 

{
	"_version": "1.14.0",
	"sap.app": {
		"id": "card.explorer.data.list.card",
		"type": "card",
		"title": "Sample for Client-Side Pagination in Cards",
		"subTitle": "Sample for Client-Side Pagination in Cards",
		"applicationVersion": {
			"version": "1.0.0"
		},
        "sap.ui5": {
            "rootView": {
                "viewName": "sap.ui.integration.sample.Destinations.Destinations",
                "type": "json",
                "async": true
            },
            "dependencies": {
                "libs": {
                    "sap.f": {},
                    "sap.ui.core": {},
                    "sap.ui.integration": {}
                }
            },
            "config": {
            }
        },        
		"shortTitle": "A short title for this Card",
		"info": "Additional information about this Card",
		"description": "A long description for this Card",
		"tags": {
			"keywords": [
				"Data",
				"Pagination",
				"Card",
				"Sample"
			]
		}
	},
	"sap.card": {
		"type": "List",
		"data": {
			"request": {
				"url": "https://services.odata.org/V4/Northwind/Northwind.svc/Products",
				"parameters": {
					"$format": "json",
					"$top": 5,
					"$count": true
				}
			},
			"path": "/value/"
		},
		"header": {
			"title": "Products",
			"subTitle": "In Stock Information",
			"icon": {
				"src": "sap-icon://product"
			},
			"status": {
				"text": "{= format.text(${i18n>CARD.COUNT_X_OF_Y}, [${parameters>/visibleItems}, ${/@odata.count}]) }"
			}
		},
		"content": {
			"item": {
				"title": "{ProductName}",
                "description": "{UnitsInStock} units in stock",
				"description1": "UnitPrice {= format.currency(${UnitPrice}, 'EUR')}",                
				"highlight": "{= ${Discontinued} ? 'Error' : 'Success'}"
			}
		}
	}
}

 

 

 

 

Deployed Samples : https://yogananda-muthaiah.github.io/UI5-Integration-Cards-samples/

Source code : https://github.com/yogananda-muthaiah/UI5-Integration-Cards-samples

Source code with simple : https://github.com/yogananda-muthaiah/ui5-integration-cards 

References : https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-you-can-prepare-your-own-template-using-sap-ui5-integration-cards/ba-p/13881521

entry.title: HANA Calculation View - Division by Zero Error
entry.content:
A short post on the fix for the Division by Zero error due to expressions in HANA calculation views

In SQL, a division by zero error occurs when an expression attempts to divide a number by zero, leading to execution failures. In HANA, this can disrupt calculations within your views, especially when working with dynamic datasets where divisor values may be unpredictable.

The Calculated column is a feature in the calculation view that helps modelers insert information based on the other columns from the data sources. 

Recently, I had to troubleshoot an issue with HANA View which was throwing an error on access - division by zero undefined: SQL Error. 

 The perennial challenge to supporting AMS team consultants is the argument from the stakeholders - "It worked before - Why is it not working now". I struggle to argue coz the opinion is not based on facts but an emotional experience with the system or software. Instead, I try to find the data points and probably share the path to resolution. 

In my case, the issue with the caulcated column. On inspection, the issue was due to a calculated column with the expression: Value/Total Value * 100. Earlier datasets never had a total value of zero.

The search for the fix for "Division by zero" error was to use NULLIF. NULLIF converts the zero to NULL which will eliminate the issue of divide by zero. 

HANA Expression editor does not facilitate NULLIF function. A forceful insertion of NULLIF in the expression resulted a Build error. 

After some attempts, I did arrive at a expression which eliminated the error. 

The expression: if("Total_Value",("Value"/ "Total_Value")*100, 0) - This expression evaluates the TOTAL VALUE column. If the column value is 0, the calculation is not performed. 

 

I find this expression mathematically simpler and less intensive on the server. 

I propose to use this logic in any calculation involving division to eliminate the possibility of Error due to Zero in the denominator. 

I hope this helps you in your data modeling. 

Regards,

Sharath

entry.title: SAP Enterprise Support Academy Newsletter November 2024
entry.content:
Dear customers,
Find here an overview of the SAP Enterprise Support Academy monthly newsletter on latest news, training courses, upcoming sessions, and further educational offerings.
The SAP Enterprise Support Academy provides expert guidance and high impact knowledge transfer services that enable you to innovate and be successful with your SAP solutions.

Subscribe to regularly receive latest news and stay informed about the wide range of educational offerings provided by the SAP Enterprise Support Academy that help you to build the digital skills tailored to your needs.

Preview available live sessions on SAP Support Portal, tailored per region:
APJ | EMEA | Latin America | North America*

Please note: A one-time registration for SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support [accessible here] is necessary to register for these events.

SAP Datasphere Security and Data Access Controls

Check out our new E-Learning on SAP Datasphere Security and Data Access Controls focusing on key items to ensure efficient management of users and roles. Taking you through global and scoped roles, SAML SSO impacts, usage of Spaces for data access control, and much more! This is a must know for SAP Datasphere administrators.

Learn more

SAP S/4HANA for Advanced Available-To-Promise

Start with our new e-learning to get an introduction to SAP S/4HANA for advanced available-to-promise. Get upskilled on topics such as product availability check, backorder processing, business process scheduling, product allocation check, and many more.

Join the new E-learning

Join SAP Enterprise Support Day 2024

Join us on November 13 or 14, for the SAP Enterprise Support Day 2024, a unique virtual event designed to provide valuable insights and strategies to elevate your business transformation through SAP’s comprehensive digital support experience. The event suits all-time zones and is available in various languages. Get registered today!

Details in the blog post or register directly

Continuous Quality Check (CQC) for Transformation Assessment

This remote service will assess the decisions made by your project team and their understanding of project challenges and tools. It offers personalized recommendations, including best practices and enablement resources for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain. To learn more about this service and how we can support your project, visit SAP Enterprise Support value maps!

Watch Video | View Blog Post

Get Empowered to Leverage AI and SAP HANA Cloud Capabilities to Innovate Faster

Join us for an exciting journey into the world of Generative AI, Joule, and SAP HANA Cloud and unlock new possibilities for your business. How? Register for our two upcoming pilots of Expert-guided Implementation services through the SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council program. The sessions are designed to empower you with the knowledge and skills to leverage SAP HANA Cloud’s advanced capabilities and AI to improve efficiency and innovation in organizations.

Read through the blog post for further details

SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council Q4 Update

Join the SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council (ESAC) program with our open workstreams for Q4. Explore our 2 new workstreams: Deployment Readiness for SAP Fieldglass & Activate Joule for SAP S/4HANA SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Benefit from collaboration, innovation, and gain early access to new tools and resources.

Further details

Lean Selective Data Transition: Your Safe and Simple way to the Intelligent Enterprise

Discover in the demo video how the SAP Business Transformation Center revolutionizes your migration to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Learn how the Lean Selective Data Transition simplifies complex data migrations, speeds up your processes, and helps you create a leaner data foundation saving both time and budget for a successful digital transformation:

Watch the video

SAP for Me Wins Best Support Websites Award!

SAP again sets the standard in Customer Support innovation, with SAP for Me recognized for its AI-powered features, personalized user experience, and seamless support services. Proud to be honored by the ASP!

Read more in the interview

How to Get Started With SAP S/4HANA Finance

Are you about to start with SAP S/4HANA Finance? Then don’t miss our interactive virtual Mee-the-Expert live session on November 15 that helps you get on board.

Register through this blog post: Get started with SAP S/4HANA Finance

Conversion to SAP S/4HANA Finance – Deep Dive

Join us in a multiday training session to discover everything you need to know about all phases and activities involved in Conversion of Accounting to SAP S/4HANA. We have two sessions in November, available on Nov 11 and 26.

More in this blog post

SAP S/4HANA Finance Fundamentals – Deep Dive

Join us in a multiday training session to discover everything you need to know about delta capabilities in SAP S/4HANA Finance. We have three sessions in November, available in English and Japanese language.

More in this blog post

In-Depth With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Series - Register Now!

We’re happy to announce that the product deep-dive enablement series “In-Depth with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition” is ready to roll out again. The series aims to provide SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition product deep-dive knowledge, independently from the release update.

Please join to get the latest update: In-Depth With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Series

Unlock Value Throughout Your Business Transformation Journey

Embracing digital transformation? It’s more than just tech upgrades. Success lies in reengineering your business model, optimizing systems, reinventing processes, and leveraging data. With tools like RISE with SAP, you can streamline cloud migration and drive innovation. Focus on collaboration, agile processes, and strategic tech integration to stay ahead and unlock new growth.

Check out the latest blog by Thomas Saueressig, member of the Executive Board of SAP SE: sap.to/61107ojAj1

Live Sessions on SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps – Continuous Quality Check Services

Join the upcoming knowledge transfer session on value maps and Continuous Quality Check (CQC) Services. CQC remote services help you to analyze a system and/or situation to reduce risks and outline optimization potential. Join this session to ask questions live and find out more about continuous quality check remote services.

Save November 14 and register here

  • SAP Enterprise Support Academy Video
  • SAP Enterprise Support Academy at a glance
  • Direct access to SAP Learning Hub (Login with your s-user ID and password)
  • SAP ES Academy Entitlement document

entry.title: SAP is a Visionary in 2024 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Enterprise Low-Code Application Platforms
entry.content:
Today, I am very proud to share the great news: Gartner has recognized SAP as a Visionary in its 2024 Magic Quadrant for Enterprise Low-Code Application Platforms (LCAP).

Let me share with you how we got here, what we believe makes us a Visionary in low-code application development with SAP Build and how you can get started with SAP’s one-stop-shop for extensibility and automations.

SAP Build – the low-code platform for SAP customers

We have designed SAP Build as the best software suite for SAP customers that want to efficiently extend their SAP solutions: SAP Build fosters fusion development allowing active collaboration between business users and professional developers, it brings Business AI with Joule copilot to boost development productivity, prebuilt LoB solutions with context-based recommendations, and deep integration with business applications.

A bit of history

Two years ago, at SAP TechEd 2022, we announced the launch of a powerful new offering to drive the next wave of business transformation by unleashing the expertise of those who know best in the lines of business.
SAP Build has been designed to put SAP’s world-class enterprise technology in the hands of multidisciplinary teams in business and IT, giving them direct, secure access to the end-to-end processes, data and context they need to make smarter decisions and drive innovation quickly. SAP Build allows business experts and, in the SAP world “Key Users”, to fruitfully collaborate with IT and developers to iterate and co-create new innovations.

Last year, at SAP TechEd 2023, we announced SAP Build Code, which went generally available in March this year. Tailored for SAP development, SAP Build Code is a comprehensive, turn-key solution for coding, testing, integrations, and application lifecycle management. Developers can leverage Joule to efficiently generate application logic, data models, and test scripts. SAP Build Code enhances the entire development process, from design time to runtime, significantly boosting developers’ productivity.

And recently at SAP TechEd 2024 we achieved another milestone by adding ABAP Environments to SAP Build: with this, SAP Build is THE extensibility solution for any SAP customer: you can now accelerate your ERP extension development and modernization, foster innovation, and automate processes, all in a single, comprehensive suite of AI-driven tools for extension development.

SAP Build is now the fastest growing area in SAP Business Technology Platform: we have doubled our revenue over the last year, increasing by more than 70% the active customer base. And we have had more than 200,000 active learners.

What makes SAP a visionary with SAP Build?

From my personal point of view, here are the key factors that make SAP Build the best development platform for SAP customers:

Fusion Team Development with one SAP Build offering

SAP is promoting the concept of fusion team development. Instead of converting pro-developers s to low-code developers (or vice versa), it combines its traditional developer stack with low-code SAP Build products. This multipronged development strategy resonates with enterprise developers and IT leaders who want the flexibility to use a range of approaches to build custom applications.

Fusion development is an enabler to be quicker with extending applications to your needs. What’s more, it is the foundation of allowing multidisciplinary teams to create connected solutions that fit the business’ needs, adds more people to the development and innovation equation which allows a much faster time to market, more fine-grained solutions shaped to the experts’ needs, and brings diversity into the innovation process.

Among our key innovations in Fusion Development:

  • Easy Project Discovery: Find, share and reuse application components with a centralized project lobby
  • Seamless Teamwork: Multiple developers can work simultaneously with version control and synchronized changes
  • Low-Code with Pro-Code Power: Business users can easily integrate pre-built solutions created by developers
  • Intuitive Iteration: Modify application, process & workspace designs and logic with drag-and drop ease, fostering collaboration between business and IT

Integrated Business AI capabilities

Assistance is what we expect from AI, to automate tedious tasks or to become faster with our assignments. Leveraging AI to speed up development productivity includes content recommendation sensitive to a certain context you find yourself in, allows you to naturally describe a process you’d like to create or even a data model to be created so you can focus on the creative parts of the app dev cycle, for instance, the UI creation. For that, SAP has integrated in SAP Build advanced AI capabilities that are grounded in business data: SAP Business AI understands business lingo and context. In addition, we recently launched at SAP TechEd the Joule Studio in SAP Build that will help customers and partners to build extensibility scenarios for Joule with custom skills and AI agents. So, Generative AI and Joule copilot are infused everywhere in SAP Build:

  • Application Development: Creation of business applications and automations
  • Process Automation: Process generation, process monitoring, document extraction and pre-built solution recommendations
  • Integration: Integration flow generation, pre-built integration recommendations
  • Joule Studio in SAP Build: Creating custom AI agents and skills for Joule copilot

Prebuilt LoB solutions

Content that allows developers to start with a ready-made template for their development is key for speeding up the creation process. Therefore, we are and have been investing heavily into content creation, be it ready-made APIs, process content packages, or application templates. Anything that is needed from a developer’s perspective to ease their work and make them faster in their creation process is available at hand in SAP Build available through the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

Both SAP and partners contribute, maintain and update pre-built content. We have now more than 500 content packages with 120+ in Finance, 115+ in Supply Chain, 40+ in Human Resources and our recent innovations in this area include, but are not limited to:

  • Finance: Capital expenditure application, automation of accrual posting and more
  • Supply Chain: Process approval for SAP IBP, Purchase requisition approval for Ariba and more
  • Human Resources: Translate job postings, position management action and more

Deep integration with business apps

SAP is unifying and standardizing its application runtimes and developer tools for numerous developer personas and use cases. This new product composition makes it easier for customers to consume SAP Build and establish partnership links. It also provides the SAP Store and SAP Business Accelerator Hub offering prebuilt reusable components that accelerate application delivery.

Recently, we have delivered:

  • Navigation from the business application UI with contextualized Extensibility Wizard to initiate an SAP Build project: available already in in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition, and soon in other SAP applications, the Extensibility Wizard is an embedded experience allowing key users to extend business applications – both on stack and side by side on SAP BTP – directly out of their business application without losing context.

For more details on how to set up the Extensibility Wizard and what extensions options are available, please read this blog post: “SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition” and watch this Devtoberfest session by @Sesh_Sreenivas and @Karthik-Thiru.

  • Extensive library of connectors, integration flows and business events for SAP and non-SAP applications in SAP Business Accelerator Hub:

Learn more and get started with SAP Build!

To learn how SAP Build can optimize your finance, procurement, human resources, and more take the next step with the following resources:

  • Visit the SAP Build product page
  • If you are using S/4HANA, visit also our SAP Build for SAP S/4HANA page
  • Discover pre-built content packages on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub
  • Explore the basic trial and start your learning journey
  • Activate your entitlements if you’re an existing RISE and GROW with SAP customer

Use SAP Build now, not only to easily create applications, but also to automate processes and to design digital workspaces with low code and generative AI!

 

 

GARTNER is a registered trademark and service mark of Gartner and Magic Quadrant is a registered trademark of Gartner, Inc. and/or its affiliates in the U.S. and internationally and are used herein with permission. All rights reserved.

This graphic was published by Gartner, Inc. as part of a larger research document and should be evaluated in the context of the entire document. The Gartner document is available upon request from SAP.

Gartner does not endorse any vendor, product or service depicted in its research publications, and does not advise technology users to select only those vendors with the highest ratings or other designation. Gartner research publications consist of the opinions of Gartner’s research organization and should not be construed as statements of fact. Gartner disclaims all warranties, expressed or implied, with respect to this research, including any warranties of merchantability or fitness for a particular purpose.

entry.title: Calling All SAP Partners: Join the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle-3 2024!
entry.content:
Attention SAP partners! We're excited to let you know that the third cycle 2024 of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative is in full swing, offering you an exclusive opportunity to collaborate with SAP on shaping the future of our products. By working closely with our developers and product managers, you'll gain early access to innovative development ideas and play a direct role in influencing SAP’s product strategy. 

This is your chance to make an impact and contribute your insights to upcoming SAP products and applications. Don't miss out - register for current projects by November 15, 2024! These projects are specifically open to SAP partners, and we look forward to hearing your valuable perspectives and ideas:

SAP S/4HANA & SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • Composable Enterprise: Reimagining Enterprise Software for Unprecedented Business Agility 
  • Help Where and When You Need It: User Assistance in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Improve your Post Upgrade Tests(PUT) Experience - An Integrated and Smart approach to PUT
  • Insightful Analytics for Actual Costing
  • Joule applications to SAP S/4HANA Utilities
  • Monitoring End to End Master Data Replications in SAP Cloud ALM
  • New Cost Settlement for real estate scenarios (Service Charge Settlement / CAM)
  • Purpose as base for processing of S/4HANA Business Partner personal data – concept and migration”
  • Research: SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit - AI: Field Mapping Proposal

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • AI Agent Builder - A Low-Code Innovation with SAP Build
  • AI-Powered Autonomous Sandbox
  • Discover SAP Build Process Automation Triggers: Empowering Experts to Automate and Extend Use Cases
  • GenAI Content Provenance and Authenticity via Watermarking
  • Knowledge Graph RAG on unstructured Data

Customer Experience

  • Redesigned SAP Sales Cloud, add-in for Microsoft Outlook

Data & Analytics

  • SAP HANA Database Migration to SAP HANA Cloud (XSC,XSA,CAP)

SAP Globalization

  • AI-supported Extension of Electronic Document Processing of SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
  • Localization as a Self-Service for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with Business AI

Lifecycle Management

  • Business Service Management in SAP Cloud ALM operations
  • Clean Core / RISE with SAP Methodology Dashboard in SAP Cloud ALM

Others

  • Early Talent Management with SAP SuccessFactors

We believe that collaboration is key to unlocking innovation. By joining forces with our customers and partners, we can collectively shape the future of SAP’s products and change the way businesses operate.

Don't delay – register today on influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 3 in 2024!

Warm regards,

Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Discover TechEd “Live” at SAPinsider EMEA 2024
entry.content:
Following hot on the heels of the hugely successful SAP TechEd Virtual event last month, attention now turns to a number of live events that will feature TechEd content and speakers, starting at the ASUG Tech Connect event in West Palm Beach, the first of SAP’s “TechEd on Tour” locations, as well as featuring in sessions at the SAPinsider EMEA “TechInsider” event in Copenhagen. Both events run from 12- 14 November in these wonderful, if quite different, locations.

I’ve been deeply involved in the planning for the SAPinsider event again, and for this reason alone will focus my blog on that event, though I am hopeful that both events will deliver a rich experience of TechEd content for attendees, and allow them to delve deeply into SAP technologies, particularly relating to SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and Artificial Intelligence (AI).

At SAPinsider Copenhagen we’re going the extra mile, in trying to deliver a TechEd-style experience for attendees, who I’m happy to say will not only find an agenda littered with TechEd sessions, delivered by many of our stellar TechEd speakers, but also they’ll be able to participate in multiple hands-on sessions as well as having an opportunity to continue their learning experience by networking with SAP’s Developer Advocates who will guide and support attendees as they work through a number of the tutorials available at the Developer Garage on the show floor – which we are pleased will make its very first appearance at this SAPinsider event.

With all this on offer I’m excited for what’s in store in Copenhagen next week – and I hope that you are too!

Plan Ahead – And Set Your Agenda Now!

For those of you registered for the event, and in a manner which is not unlike visiting a Disney theme park, my suggestion for you to get the most out of an SAPinsider event is to plan ahead – not so much to beat the queues as you might at the theme park, but more so to make sure that you see all you want to see, and achieve what you set out to do. And the best way to do this is to prepare your agenda for the event before you go.

The first step is of course to get the event app, “SAPinsider Events”, available on the App Store and Google Play, and once you’ve installed that you’re good to go in terms of adding sessions to your personal agenda. Now for the fun bit – what sessions to see? Perhaps I might help with some ideas.

I’ll assume that everyone will plan to join the day 1 and day 2 keynotes. I’m looking forward to both, the motivational first day keynote from Hein Wagner, starting at 9.00am on Tuesday 12th November, followed the next day by the AI inspired second day keynote, featuring Martin Böhm of Ottobock and Jonathan Von Rüden of SAP, and which starts at the same time of day on the 13th November.

But after the keynotes, what next? Here are my personal 6 TechEd inspired picks for Copenhagen:

  1. Enhancing SAP Development Processes with SAP Build and ABAP Cloud Interoperability to Elevate Your Extensions, 12-Nov-24, 10:30 - 11:15
  2. Hands-On Lab: Develop Applications and Processes with SAP Build and Joule, 12-Nov-24, 15:30 - 17:30
  3. Mastering SAP Business AI for Enhanced Performance and Innovation, 13-Nov-24, 10:30 - 11:15
  4. SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere Solutions: Integration, 13-Nov-24, 14:15 - 15:00
  5. Clean Core Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, 13-Nov-24, 16:45 - 17:30
  6. SAP BTP Strategy and Highlights, 14-Nov-24, 09:00 - 09:45

Of course, this is just the tip of the iceberg, as there are many more sessions available for you to select from the agenda, and among which you’ll find more TechEd content, as well as content from other speakers from SAP and our business partners, in addition to many customer case stories too. And in a shameless plug too, you may also note my session “Navigating Your Path to Success with SAP BTP” which I shall deliver alongside Nis Boy Naeve on 13 November at 11:30am. Please join us if you can! Take a look at the SAPinsider event website sessions list, or the event app that you’ve already downloaded to see more.

Your Continued Learning Experience

If you’d like to continue your own personal learning journey after the event, then why not explore other learning opportunities from SAP? For example, learn how to build software applications, side-by-side extensions, and integrations to and from cloud applications by exploring SAP’s free learning content on SAP BTP. It is made for both integration designers and extension developers from all levels of expertise and will help you stay up to date with the latest SAP BTP innovations. And check out even more role-based learning resources and opportunities to get certified in one place on SAP Learning site.

Or if you’re interested in TechEd sessions, but can’t join the events in West Palm Beach or Copenhagen, then perhaps another TechEd on Tour location, in Australia, Germany or the UK would work better for you?

For those of you travelling to Copenhagen next week, I do hope that you find SAPinsider EMEA 2024 to be a truly informative and stimulating experience. And, of course, if you visit the show floor then look me up at the SAP exhibit booth, and I’ll be happy to have a chat with you about SAP BTP, the conference or just about the amazing city of Copenhagen!

My best wishes for safe travels, and I hope that you enjoy the event!

Chris Grundy

Product Marketing, SAP BTP

entry.title: 🎄 Get into the holiday spirit with our SAP Build & AGILITA Workshop! ✨
entry.content:
Are you an SAP customer interested in SAP S/4HANA Extensibility or SAP Business AI use cases?

If yes, plan a visit to Berlin end of this month, and get into the holiday spirit with our very special SAP Build & AGILITA Workshop!

We are happy to offer you:

  • Product deep dives: Jumpstart your SAP Build product knowledge with a hands-on exercise, including a gen-AI scenario connected to SAP S/4HANA for application development.
  • Discussions with experts: Gain valuable insights about our portfolio and strategy, while sharing your questions with our technical experts.
  • Christmas Market Fun: Explore SAP Build solutions in an authentic Christmas Market, where you can get to know the products and teams, along with some warm glühwein and waffles!

 🎄 This isn’t your everyday workshop, as we’ve planned a unique experience with a festive holiday theme to go along with your introduction to SAP Build! ✨
 

DATE

📅  November 28 - 11:00am - 7:00pm CET

LOCATION

📍 Berlin

WHO SHOULD ATTEND

Developers, team leads, and also business users interested in extending core processes with SAP Build.

AGENDA

Part 1 - SAP Berlin office

  • 11:00am - Lunch & networking
  • 11:30am - Opening keynotes + Q&A
  • 12:15pm - Product deep dives + Q&A
  • 1:00pm - Hands-on exercise
  • 4:00pm - Transportation to AGILITA office (provided by SAP)

Part 2 - AGILITA office

  • 4:30pm - Christmas Market
  • 7:00pm - Closing remarks

JOIN US!

If you’re interested, please register here and we will get back to you in a few hours to confirm your participation 👉

 

On a personal note, I'm very excited to be part of this special event, which will be a unique opportunity to engage with SAP customers... and also to catch up with dear colleagues I haven't seen in person for a long time. I'm also looking forward to meeting people from our valued partner AGILITA, whom I interviewed a year and a half ago.

So, see you in Berlin on 28 November?

entry.title: Unlocking the Power of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) in AI: A Game Changer for SAP Customers
entry.content:
The rapid progression in artificial intelligence (AI), especially with large language models (LLMs), is transforming how businesses approach intelligent decision-making. Yet, as promising as these technologies are, challenges such as hallucination—where AI generates incorrect or irrelevant information—can limit their effectiveness in real-world business applications.

One of the key SAP innovations overcoming this issue is Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). By combining LLMs with real-time data retrieval, RAG provides a powerful solution for generating reliable, accurate insights.

In this blog, we’ll dive into RAG, its importance, and how SAP is empowering its customers to make the most of this cutting-edge technology.

What Exactly is Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)?

RAG offers a breakthrough by blending generative AI models with a dynamic data retrieval process. When posed with a query, the AI doesn’t just rely on pre-trained knowledge. Instead, it actively fetches the most relevant, up-to-date information from databases or document repositories, resulting in responses that are both accurate and contextually grounded.

This hybrid approach greatly reduces the risks of hallucinations and ensures that the AI’s outputs are not only trustworthy but also highly applicable to real-time business scenarios.

The Two Pillars of RAG:

  • Retrieval: This component involves the model retrieving the most pertinent data or documents in response to a query.
  • Generation: Using that retrieved information, the model generates a coherent and contextually relevant answer.

By integrating real-time data into the generation process, businesses can ensure that their AI systems provide insightful, accurate, and timely responses.

Why Do We Need RAG?

While LLMs have revolutionized the use of AI for deriving insights, their limitations in practical business applications are well-known:

  • Hallucinations: LLMs may present factually inaccurate or irrelevant responses when detached from current data.
  • Cost of Fine-Tuning: Customizing models with domain-specific knowledge requires significant time, effort, and cost.
  • Stagnant Knowledge: Once trained, LLMs don’t automatically update, limiting their value in fast-paced industries.

By embedding a retrieval mechanism, RAG addresses these limitations. It ensures that AI-generated responses are not only relevant but also backed by the most recent and accurate information available.

Why is RAG Crucial for SAP’s Customers?

Industries such as supply chain management, human resources, and customer relationship management, where SAP operates, thrive on accurate, up-to-date information. Implementing RAG within these sectors offers:

  • Reliable insights: By grounding AI outputs in the latest business data, responses are more trustworthy and useful.
  • Faster response times: RAG enables AI-driven systems to pull relevant information quickly, streamlining business operations.
  • Cost efficiency: Businesses can use RAG with existing data repositories instead of engaging in costly LLM fine-tuning.

SAP’s Approach to RAG

SAP is actively incorporating RAG into its platform, providing users with the tools to build AI systems tailored to their specific needs. Here’s how SAP is enabling this:

  • Centralized RAG Service: SAP’s scalable, domain-agnostic RAG service integrates smoothly with existing user interfaces. This service ensures that AI models leverage the most up-to-date information while also offering transparency by tracing the sources of data used in responses.
  • Vector Search Engine in SAP HANA Cloud: SAP’s advanced vector engine efficiently stores and queries data embeddings, making retrieval faster and more efficient.

Document Grounding in SAP’s Joule and AI Services

SAP’s AI services, including Joule—an AI copilot—are enhancing their capabilities through RAG:

  • Indexing & Retrieval: Documents are transformed into vector embeddings, which are stored and retrieved for accurate, contextual responses.
  • Grounded Generation: The retrieved data informs AI-generated answers, significantly improving accuracy and relevance.

Real-World Impact: RAG in SAP SuccessFactors

One of the most promising applications of RAG within SAP’s ecosystem is in the realm of HR, specifically within SAP SuccessFactors. Typically, answering HR-related queries could take up to 20 minutes per question, putting a strain on HR teams.

With RAG integrated into tools like Joule, employees can now access relevant policies instantly. By querying policy documents directly, the system retrieves relevant sections, generating accurate answers in seconds. This has led to a 35% reduction in the volume of HR inquiries.

Conclusion

The introduction of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) is revolutionizing how businesses can harness AI for more accurate, grounded, and reliable insights.

SAP’s integration of this technology into platforms like Joule and BTP allows its customers to take full advantage of AI-driven decision-making without the high costs and complexity of fine-tuning models. As a result, SAP customers are poised to unlock significant value by adopting RAG in their day-to-day operations.

entry.title: Join Us in Shaping the Future with the SAP Business Technology Platform!
entry.content:
At SAP, we believe that collaboration is key to driving innovation. We’re excited to invite our valued customers and partners to engage in a series of groundbreaking projects leveraging the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). This powerful platform integrates data management, analytics, application development, and intelligent technologies, enabling you to unlock new possibilities for your business.

Why Get Involved?

Participating in these projects allows you to harness the full potential of the SAP BTP. With its low-code development capabilities, cloud security features, and AI-driven solutions, the platform empowers you to streamline operations and enhance decision-making. Your insights and expertise are crucial in shaping tools that align with your unique needs and challenges.

Explore Our Upcoming Projects

We’re focusing on a variety of innovative initiatives built on the capabilities of SAP BTP, including:

  • Low-Code Development: Simplify application creation and process automation to enhance agility.
  • Cloud Security Enhancements: Strengthen your security posture with advanced encryption and key management solutions.
  • AI-Powered Insights: Leverage AI to transform unstructured data into actionable insights.
  • Automation and Integration: Discover how to automate processes seamlessly across your organization.

How to Participate

If you’re interested in getting involved, we’d love to hear from you! Reach out to us to express your interest in participating in these exciting initiatives. Together, we can leverage the SAP Business Technology Platform to drive innovation and create impactful solutions that help your organization thrive.

Thank you for being part of our journey. We look forward to collaborating with you to shape the future of technology!

Register until November 15, 2024 and take advantage of this opportunity!

SAP Business Technology Platform projects for registration:

 

  • GenAI Content Provenance and Authenticity via Watermarking
  • Investigation into a Customer Security Dashboard
  • AI-Powered Autonomous Sandbox
  • Discover SAP Build Process Automation Triggers: Empowering Experts to Automate and Extend Use Cases
  • Cloud Security: Improving the Encryption Key Management Experience
  • Knowledge Graph RAG on unstructured Data
  • AI Agent Builder - A Low-Code Innovation with SAP Build

 

Visit the Customer Influence website on influence.sap.com to register.

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

If you are also interested in other CEI projects that are currently open for registration, see here the full list.

Warm regards,

Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team

entry.title: Creating a Dashboard from Address Cleansing Results using SAP Data Quality Management Microservices
entry.content:
In my last blog post, I introduced our GitHub sample for working with SAP Datasphere and SAP Data Quality Management, microservices. Today we are announcing our new sample to show how you might create a data quality dashboard from your address cleansing and geocoding results. If you are not familiar with our GitHub sample structure, please refer to the previous article first.

 

Dashboard sample using the result from SAP Data Quality Management, microservices for location data

Our sample can be found in the SAP-samples / cloud-dqm-sample-payloads repository.

/ datasphere-dashboard

This sample uses the additional details generated during the address cleansing and geocoding process to create a statistical visualization using SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud.

 

Data Quality Dashboard

In SAP Analytics Cloud, you can create a dashboard to visualize the total number of records for various informational codes received from SAP Data Quality Management microservices for location data. In our example, we used the pie and bar chart representations as shown below.

 

Output Field Properties

SAP Data Quality Management, microservices for location data generates several Informational codes that can be used to create reports. These codes indicate how well the address data was matched to the reference data and provide additional information if the best possible results are not achieved. To generate these codes, you must include the informational codes as output field properties in each request to the service.

The sample dashboard above was created using some of these output fields, but you can create your own reports using any output field properties as described in the documentation: Assignment Codes and Identify an Invalid Address.

 

Address Validation Summary

The output field addr_asmt_info provides the information about the validity of the address. If it is V (Valid) or C (Corrected), it is considered that the address returned is validated to the full depth in the reference data. If it is I (Invalid) or B (Blank), you may be able to remedy these addresses by enabling the Suggestion Lists properties.

Valid

V (Valid)

Address was validated based on the depth of reference data and received no changes or only minor changes

C (Corrected)

Address was corrected during the validation process and was validated based on the depth of reference data

Invalid

I (Invalid)

Address did not not match to the full depth of reference data

B (Blank)

The request had blank values in all address input field properties

 

Address Validation Level

The output field addr_asmt_level provides the level that the address matches to the address reference data.

Premise

S (Secondary)

Address was validated to the secondary address information such as a floor and unit number

PR (Primary Range)

Address was validated to the house number

Street

PN (Street)

Address was validated to the street

City

L4 (Locality4)

Address was validated to the fourth level of city

L3 (Locality3)

Address was validated to the third level of city

L2 (Locality4)

Address was validated to the second level of city

L1 (Locality1)

Address was validated to the first level of city

Region

R (Region)

Address was validated to the region

C (Country)

Address was validated to the country

Unassigned

X (Unassigned)

Address was not validated to any level of data

 

Geocode Validation Level

The output field geo_asmt_level provides the level that the address matches to the geocode reference data.

Premise

PRE (Primary Range Exact)

Address was geocoded to the exact house location

PRI (Primary Range Interpolated)

Address was geocoded to the interpolated location based on the house number range

Street

PN (Primary Name)

Address was geocoded to the midpoint of a street segment

Postcode

PF (Postcode Full)

Address was geocoded to the center of a full postcode area

P2P (Postcode2 Partial)

Address was geocoded to the center of a partial postcode2 area

P1 (Postcode1)

Address was geocoded to the center of a postcode1 area

City

L3 (Locality3)

Address was geocoded to the center of a third-level city area

L2 (Locality2)

Address was geocoded to the center of a second-level city area

L1 (Locality1)

Address was geocoded to the center of a first-level city area

Unassigned

X (Unassigned)

Address was not geocoded to any level of data

 

Reasons for Invalid Addresses

The output fields addr_info_code and addr_info_code_msg provide the potential reasons why an address was not fully validated to the full depth in the reference data. You can find the list of codes and associated messages in the documentation: Information Codes for Address Cleanse. You may be able to remedy these addresses by enabling the Suggestion Lists properties.

Information Code

1000’s

Potential problems related to the input country or script

2000’s

Potential problems related to the input city, region, or postcode

3000’s

Potential problems related to the input street, building, house number, or postbox number

4000’s

Potential problems related to the input secondary address information or organization name

5000’s

Miscellaneous scenarios encountered when matching the input address to reference data. Not all of these scenarios are necessarily problems

 

We Value Your Feedback!

In this blog post, we showed an example of interpreting the output data generated by SAP Data Quality Management, microservices for location data, creating the data model in SAP Datasphere, and visualizing the data to create a data quality dashboard.

If you like what we did in our sample, we would appreciate your feedback on it. Is there any additional information you would like to get from the service? Do you want to see similar reports from the Data Quality Service UI? If you would like to send us an enhancement request, please use the following channel to contact us.

  • Enhancement requests to SAP Data Quality Management, microservices for location data
  • Enhancement requests to SAP Datasphere
  • Enhancement requests to SAP Analytics Cloud
  • Enhancement requests to SAP S/4HANA Cloud for MDM

entry.title: How to Check for Custom Domains in Your Subaccount
entry.content:
Introduction

You might be going through one of the following scenarios in the SAP BTP, Neo environment: 

  • You want to move the custom domain setup for one of your applications from one subaccount X in global account A to a different subaccount Y in another global account B within the same region. See How to Migrate a Custom Domain Between Global Accounts. 
  • You want to move a subaccount X with existing custom domains in global account A to another global account B within the same region. See How to Move a Subaccount with Existing Custom Domains to Another Global Account. 
  • You want to remove a custom domain that you no longer need. See Remove the Custom Domain. 
  • You want to delete a subaccount to clean up your account hierarchy, free up quota used by services in that subaccount, or to reduce overall costs. See Delete a Subaccount. 

Even if your case is different from the ones mentioned above, it’s always good to know whether you’re actively using any custom domains.

 

Prerequisites

To verify that you have a custom domain configured in your subaccount in the SAP BTP, Neo environment, make sure that you fulfill the following requirements: 

  • To be able to run any console commands in the Neo environment, install the SDK according to the instructions in Install the SAP BTP SDK for Neo Environment, if you haven’t done that until now. 
  • You are a member of the subaccount.

 

Procedure 

Run the following command: 

neo list-ssl-hosts --account <subaccount_technical_name> --user <e-mail_or_username> --host <host> 

 

Command Input Example: 

neo list-ssl-hosts --account ab123c4d5 –user mymail@example.com --host hana.ondemand.com  

 

Command Output Example 1: 

No SSL hosts found for subaccount ab123c4d5

 

If you receive this message in the command output, it means that you don't have any configured custom domains in the subaccount. 

 

Command Output Example 2: 

Name : mySSLhost1 

Host : EU1234.ssl.ondemand.com  

Certificate : myCert1

 

Name : mySSLhost2 

Host : EU5678.ssl.ondemand.com  

Certificate : myCert2

 

In the second example, you have two custom domain configurations in the subaccount.

 

Related Information

list-ssl-hosts 

list-custom-domain-mappings 

SAP Note 3246456 - Moving a subaccount to a different global account

entry.title: SAP Fiori development newsletter November 2024 (issue #31)
entry.content:
If you are like us, you are already thinking about your plans for 2025 and prioritizing topics. What technologies to embrace and which to de-prioritize? Which apps will deliver the biggest returns for the business units with whom you work?

At SAP, we are thinking about what innovations will make it easier for you to build SAP Fiori apps and how to integrate AI into the development process as well as the SAP Fiori elements framework.

Our next SAP Fiori Innovation Day is in Heidelberg on Tuesday, November 19 will focus on the intersection between AI and SAP Fiori. A few seats remain, so please register soon if you are interested in learning about the intersection between AI and SAP Fiori.

We are planning our 2025 SAP Fiori Innovation Day events. Please let us know if you are interested in joining us as we bring our team of SAP experts and partners to a city near you. 

If you want to meet with fellow developers and the SAP engineers who build our professional development tools, I encourage you to join our monthly SAP Fiori elements and SAP Fiori tools roundtable group. Our next meeting is Wednesday, November 13, 2024. You can also email me if you would like an invitation.

If you prefer to receive this in your inbox, please subscribe to the SAP Fiori development newsletter.

Since this is our last issue of 2024, we would like to extend our best wishes for a happy holiday season.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Development News

Introducing the new Adaptation Editor experience for SAPUI5 adaptation projects

To improve the development experience and harmonize the editors for SAPUI5 adaptation projects offered in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) and Visual Studio Code, BAS now features the new Adaptation Editor, replacing the SAPUI5 Visual Editor.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/introducing-the-new-adaptation-editor-experience-for-sapui5-adaptation/ba-p/13923903

Explore SAPUI5 freestyle development with Joule

Joule in SAP Build Code supports you with generative AI features to speed up SAPUI5 app development. Explore step-by-step guidance, AI-driven code suggestions, and advanced formatting options to bring your projects to life quickly and efficiently.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/ui5-freestyle-development-in-sap-build-code-with-joule/ba-p/13897852

Product updates for SAP Build Code – October 2024 edition

SAP Build Code brings powerful enhancements for SAP Fiori developers. With Joule's AI-powered code search and in-line coding assistance, you can quickly find code snippets and get smart suggestions. Plus, streamline your UI customization with the new Adaptation Editor for SAPUI5 projects, and ensure good code quality with UI5 Linter v1.0. Discover more SAP Build Code updates:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/product-updates-for-sap-build-code-october-2024-edition/ba-p/13923399

UI5 linter v1.0 now available

The UI5 team is happy to announce the official release of UI5 linter v1.0, a static code analysis tool for UI5 projects. Over the past few months, valuable community feedback has been incorporated and the tool has been enhanced with additional detection features. Explore the improvements in this blog post:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/release-of-ui5-linter-v1-0/ba-p/13880556

Smart choices for your UI: When to use sap.ui.mdc

sap.ui.mdc is a specialized library within SAPUI5. It is designed to enhance user experience with advanced controls and features, helping you build metadata-driven controls (like smart controls) and applications. This post describes some scenarios where it might be the right choice for your project.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/smart-choices-for-your-ui-when-to-use-sap-ui-mdc/ba-p/13869819

From JavaScript to TypeScript: how we enhanced code reliability in SAPUI5 development

We have published several articles on the advantages of TypeScript. This post describes one team’s journey to improve their code quality.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/1-3-from-javascript-to-typescript-how-we-enhanced-code-reliability-in/ba-p/13878726

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/2-3-from-javascript-to-typescript-how-we-enhanced-code-reliability-in/ba-p/13925918

Events

SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Heidelberg on November 19

After our successful events in Dublin and Chicago, we are bringing SAP Fiori Innovation Day back to Europe. The next one will be in Heidelberg on November 19. By attending this event, you will learn:

  • SAP’s UX strategy and how it is evolving
  • Recent UX innovations that fulfill the promise of the intelligent, collaborative enterprise
  • Making SAP Fiori work for you – finding the apps you need and adapting them to your business requirements
  • Building apps using the latest tools and technologies – the same ones SAP uses to build apps for SAP S/4HANA
  • How to move your organization forward, including how to take advantage of AI for developing applications

Register today:
https://events.sap.com/eur/sap-fiori-innovation-day-heidelberg-2024/en/home

SAP Fiori Innovation Days 2025

We are planning our 2025 SAP Fiori Innovation Day schedule. Please let us know which location you might be interested in joining us.

We will not have events in every location, but we want to get a sense of where you are willing to travel to learn about SAP Fiori app development (see topics above).
https://events.sap.com/sap-fiori-innovation-day-2025/en/home

SAP TechEd recap

There were many announcements at SAP TechEd 2024. Here is a consolidated and simplified digest.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-teched-2024-announcements-consolidated-and-simplified/ba-p/13900729

UI5ers Live – November 14

The UI5 team will explore AI-powered SAPUI5 coding with Joule, and the Test Starter, a new concept to streamline test setup for OpenUI5 in the upcoming edition of UI5ers live:

Register here: https://openui5.org/events/#id=ui5ers-43

Steer the future of SAP Build at the SAP BTP Usability Event

Would you like to help steer the future direction of SAP Build products like SAP Build Work Zone, CDS-Modeler, SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Process Automation, UI5?  If so, please join us on November 25-26, 2024 at our headquarters in Walldorf, where you can:

  • Gain early insights into SAP's product vision, ideas, and research projects
  • Share ideas directly with the development teams and influence product development
  • Participate in the development of products that better meet your needs and help your business
  • Provide valuable input on new features directly to the engineering teams during 1:1 usability testing sessions
  • Access expert talks and networking opportunities

To register, contact Marina Kellermann (standard SAP email format) before November 15. She can send you all the details around the full agenda, accommodations, etc.

Tutorials

Tutorial: SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects for SAP S/4HANA Cloud

SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects in SAP Business Application Studio are not new. What is new, is the support for SAP S/HANA Cloud. This enables developers to enhance SAPUI5 applications in SAP S/4HANA Cloud with features that were previously only for on-premise customers.
